official.name: Brad Little
official.office_address: Office of the Governor, Boise, Idaho

summary.narrative: Gov. Little emphasizes fiscal discipline, tax relief, education investment, workforce development, and government efficiency. Second-term accomplishments include $4.6B tax cuts, largest school funding increase in state history, $2B facility funding, and red tape reduction initiatives. Currently implementing 'Enduring Idaho' and 'Keeping Promises' plans for 2026 re-election bid.
summary.grade_rationale: Strong delivery on education and tax relief commitments with measurable outcomes; demonstrated legislative effectiveness; limited transparency on school choice standards and long-term outcome tracking reduces grade from A range.

promises.normalized: Deliver $4.6 billion in tax cuts over six years
Secure single largest school funding increase in state history
Allocate $2 billion over 10 years for school facility projects
Implement fair, responsible school choice prioritizing families in need

data_gaps: Specific workforce development program outcomes and metrics
Details on red tape reduction quantification and impact assessment
School choice proposal status and implementation timeline
Long-term economic growth data supporting 'Enduring Idaho' plan
Teacher pay increase specifics and salary benchmarking
Literacy investment program results and assessment data
Current unemployment and wage growth rates in Idaho
Federal grant matching or contribution to education initiatives
Impact of firing squad legislation on capital punishment outcomes
